Legacy numeric job parser that also accepts v3 import_job_id (UUID string).
If `job_id` looks numeric it follows the legacy parse flow; otherwise it
delegates to the v3 `parse_job_v3` handler using the UUID import_job_id.

get.description:
Legacy numeric import getter that also accepts v3 import_job_id UUIDs.
Numeric ids follow the legacy path; UUID strings are delegated to the
v3 `get_import_job` helper which returns the v3 shaped job metadata.

post.description:
Legacy numeric map endpoint that also accepts v3 import_job_id UUIDs.
If given a UUID, delegate to the v3 `map_v3` handler.

Legacy numeric validate endpoint that also accepts v3 import_job_id UUIDs.
Delegate to v3 `validate_v3` when a UUID is supplied.

Legacy numeric commit endpoint that also accepts v3 import_job_id UUIDs.
For UUIDs, delegate to the v3 commit compatibility helper which will
attempt to locate and commit via the v3 path.

Compatibility helper: given a v3 `import_job_id` attempt to locate the
legacy numeric import_job and invoke the legacy commit logic so callers
using v3 ids can complete a commit immediately.

get.description: Return roles and effective permissions for a given user id.

post.description:
Compute financial ROI.

Expected payload keys:
  - commit_fund (Cost)
  - adj_contracts (Adjusted contracts)
  - value_per_contract (optional override)
  - vpc_lookup (optional): if present, ignored here (future)

Compute ROI_Score (Benchmark Index Option A).

Inputs (preferred): cost, impressions, engagements, adj_leads, appt_made, appt_conduct, adj_contracts, objective, tactic
Benchmarks are read from `cost_benchmark` (for cost KPIs) and `conversion_benchmark` for conversion baselines.
If benchmark rows are not present, KPI_band scoring or conversion indexes will gracefully return null/na values.

get.summary: List LOEs assigned to a priority

  - evidence: existing evidence rows
  - assumptions: inferred assumptions when inputs are sparse

get.description:
Return a ranked list of school opportunity scores (deterministic, explainable).

Scoring components (weights):
  - population (40%) : larger populations favored
  - availability rate (30%) : proportion of available students
  - recent funnel activity (30%) : aggregated counts from `fact_funnel`

The router attempts to match `fact_funnel.org_unit_id` to either the
`school_program_fact.id` or `school_program_fact.rsid_prefix` when aggregating
funnel activity.

Deterministic recommendation engine layered on opportunity scores.

Recommendation classes:
  - prioritize_now
  - monitor
  - deprioritize

Rules (deterministic):
  - If score >= 50 -> prioritize_now, action_priority=high
  - If 25 <= score < 50 -> monitor, action_priority=medium
  - If score < 25 -> deprioritize, action_priority=low

Engagement focus is suggested from dominant factor mix.

Deterministic mission-risk engine for units.

Uses `fact_funnel` to compute lead volume and a proxy conversion rate
(appointments / leads using stage name matching). Higher risk is
assigned when lead volume is low and conversion is weak.

Rank market ZIPs (or markets) using `mi_zip_fact` as source.

Factors used (deterministic):
  - potential_remaining (primary)
  - army_share (secondary)
  - p2p (tertiary)

Scores are relative within the current query result set and returned
with an explicit reason and suggested_focus.

Rank events using `event_metrics` and simple conversion proxies.

Factors used: engagement_count, conversion proxies (attendees->leads), recent ROI if available.

Rank marketing campaigns using `fact_marketing`.

Factors used: engagement rate, conversion rate, cost proxies if available.

get.description:
Lightweight metrics endpoint.

Priority order:
1. If a stored `metric_definition` exists, execute its `sql_definition`.
2. Otherwise proxy known metric_ids to tactical_rollups for compatibility.
Return a canonical budget rollup: kpis (allocated/obligated/expended), breakdowns by funding_line, trends by fy/qtr.
Return project rollup: counts, planned/actual costs, breakdowns by org_unit or funding_line.
Return events rollup: counts, budget sums, event metrics aggregation.

post.description:
Find duplicate business-key records and archive older ones.

This is a safe, idempotent operation: it will mark duplicates as archived
(set `record_status='archived'` and `archived_at`) while keeping one
active row per business key.

post.summary: Purge archived records older than X days
post.description:
Purge archived records older than `days`. Defaults to 90.

`tables` can be provided to restrict which tables to purge; otherwise a
conservative default set is used.

Lightweight scoring endpoint used by the lightweight frontend demo.

Returns a mocked score and recommendation derived from simple heuristics.

get.description: Return the deterministic importer registry used by the ingest pipeline.

Return simple market recommendations derived from market_zip_metrics.

This provides a lightweight compatibility shim for clients expecting
`/api/v2/ai/recommendations/markets`.

Return status info for known connectors (EMM, Vantage, AIE).
Derive availability from importer registry, loader presence, and DB tables.

post.description:
Trigger an on-demand EMM import based on the latest EMM upload known to the system.

Behavior:
- locate most recent import_file linked to an import_job_v3 with source_system or filename suggesting EMM
- run importer_registry.detect_importer and importer_registry.run_import
- create/update an import_run row and update import_job_v3.summary_json

Simple compatibility endpoint to mark a lead as contacted.

Body: { lead_id: <id>, status?: 'contacted', note?: 'quick note' }
This attempts to update common lead columns if present, and will
add a `status` column if the schema doesn't contain one (best-effort).

get.description:
List leads with simple server-side pagination and filtering.

Query params supported:
  - school_id
  - zip5
  - status
  - limit (page size)
  - offset

Returns: { status: 'ok', total: <int>, rows: [ ... ] }

Return a lightweight planning overview (compatibility for /api/v2/planning/overview).
This delegates to the existing planning_summary.projects_events_list() where available
and maps results to a simple `items` array the frontend expects.
